motivation: The user wanted the project installed and runnable in the current machine environment, enabling development and execution of the built CLI.
approach: Installed the missing Bun runtime (v1.3.11) and made it available on PATH via ~/bin/bun, then ran `bun install` and `bun run build` to produce the compiled output and verified the resulting CLI by running `dist/cli.js --version` under both Node and Bun.
challenges: The environment initially lacked Bun, which was addressed by installing it and exposing the executable for the current user.
tradeoffs: Chose a user-local Bun install (~/bin) rather than a system-wide installation to avoid requiring elevated/system changes.
outcome: Project dependencies were installed, the build succeeded with artifacts generated in `dist/`, and runtime validation confirmed the CLI runs correctly under both Node and Bun. No repository files were modified.
